input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known issue. In .NET MAUI / Android, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ and formally removed/ignored in newer versions. The modern replacement is using `edge-to-edge` display with proper `WindowInsets` handling.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer about the .NET 10 / Android approach.


In .NET 10, the `Resize` soft input mode is no longer effective because Android 11+ moved to an edge-to-edge model where apps need to handle `WindowInsets` directly to respond to the keyboard appearing. The best approach is either switching to `Pan` mode or implementing proper inset handling through MAUI's keyboard management or Android's `WindowInsetsCompat`. SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and Android 15 enforces edge-to-edge by default, which breaks this behavior entirely—especially since .NET 10 targets Android 15 by default. The main solutions involve switching to `AdjustPan`, using `WindowInsetsAnimation` to listen for keyboard changes and adjust padding, or leveraging `ImeInsets` with `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows`. For a chat screen specifically, I'd set up edge-to-edge mode in the Android platform code and listen for keyboard insets to add bottom padding dynamically, or just use `AdjustPan` as the quickest fix.
